issues.title: test(backlog-triage): integration test for triage-apply against a disposable scratch repo
issues.body:
Part of #59 (epic). Deferred from #65 per its AC: "Automated integration test against a disposable scratch repo is **DEFERRED** to a follow-up issue; MVP ships with unit-level apply-parse tests."

## Scope

Add an opt-in integration test that exercises `triage-apply.js --apply --yes` against a real (disposable) scratch repo. Shipped MVP (#65) covers:

- `parseReport` / `dedupActions` / `toGhCommands` — pure, unit-tested
- orchestration — tested with an injected `runGh` stub
- dry-run pipeline — tested against a canonical fixture report
- idempotency via JSONL apply log — tested by seeding the log

What's NOT tested: actual `gh` invocation side effects on GitHub (label mutations, milestone assignments, issue closes, comment bodies). This follow-up closes that gap.

## Approach

- Create a disposable test repo (e.g., `sungjunlee/triage-apply-sandbox`) with a known baseline: a handful of open issues with labels, milestones, and bodies.
- Run \`triage-apply.js --apply --yes\` against a pre-rendered triage report that points at those issues.
- After the run, read issue state via \`gh issue list --json\` and assert:
  - close / close-duplicate actions flipped issues to CLOSED with the expected comment + resolution reason
  - set-priority removed the old priority label and added the new one (no duplicate \`priority:\\*\` labels remain)
  - assign-milestone set the milestone correctly
  - revisit added a comment but left the issue OPEN
- Reset the sandbox to baseline via a teardown step (reopen issues, restore labels).

## Gating

- Opt-in only — runs under a \`TRIAGE_APPLY_INTEGRATION=1\` env flag, NOT by default in \`node --test\`.
- Requires a \`GH_TOKEN\` with write access to the sandbox repo.
- Skip the test with a clear message if the env flag or token is missing.

## Why deferred

Real-repo integration tests require a sandbox repo + credentials + teardown discipline that's out of scope for the #65 MVP. The decision layer unit tests + injected-runner orchestration tests already cover the argv composition and control flow — what's missing is end-to-end verification that the emitted argv actually does what we expect on GitHub's side.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Integration test file \`skills/backlog-triage/scripts/triage-apply.integration.test.js\`
- [ ] Guarded by \`TRIAGE_APPLY_INTEGRATION=1\` env flag; skips cleanly without it
- [ ] Uses a dedicated disposable sandbox repo (documented in the test file)
- [ ] Covers all 5 MVP verbs: close, revisit, close-duplicate, set-priority, assign-milestone
- [ ] Teardown reopens issues + restores labels so the test is rerunnable
- [ ] README + SKILL.md updated with how to run the integration test (env flag, token requirement)

issues.title: enhance(backlog-triage): snapshot v2 — closing_prs via GraphQL + optional comments
issues.body:
Follow-up to #59 epic. Surfaced during Batch 2 reviews (#62, #63) — both scripts legitimately need data that the MVP snapshot doesn't carry.

## Why now

- #62's "PR linkage" signal (merged PR → open issue) requires each snapshot issue to carry its closing-PR references. Not in MVP snapshot.
- #62's "Mentions in comments" signal requires comments on each issue. Not in MVP snapshot.
- #63's "scanMergedPR" and "scanDuplicateOfClosed" signals need the same data.

Shipped Batch 2 MVPs (#62, #63 after re-dispatch) cover body-scan + keyword + label signals — about 80% of the surface. This issue covers the remaining 20%.

## Scope

Extend `scripts/triage-collect.js`:

1. **`closing_prs` via single GraphQL query** (hard requirement — single `gh` fetch preserved)
   - Migrate the default fetch from `gh issue list --json` to `gh api graphql` with a query returning open issues + their `closedByPullRequestsReferences(first:5, includeClosedPrs:true)` nodes
   - Per-issue key: `closing_prs: [{number, state, mergedAt, url}]`; empty array when no closing PR linked
   - Paginate via GraphQL cursor if repo has > default-page-size open issues; keep it under one command invocation at the CLI level

2. **Optional `--with-comments` flag** (per-issue fetch; breaks single-fetch contract behind an explicit opt-in)
   - When enabled, adds `comments: [{author, body, createdAt}]` to each issue
   - Default off; document the per-issue fetch cost in `references/classification.md`
   - Surface N API calls warning when active

3. **Optional `--with-closed-issues` flag** (second list query; supports duplicate-of-closed detection in #63)
   - Adds top-level `closed_issues: [{number, title, body, closedAt}]` with the same filter bounds as open
   - Limit to closed within last 180 days by default; configurable

## Downstream plan

After this ships:
- **#62 follow-up**: implement PR-linkage edge kind + comment-mention scan (behind `--with-comments` snapshot requirement)
- **#63 follow-up**: implement `scanMergedPR` + `scanDuplicateOfClosed` scanners, remove deferred-stub markers

These can be tracked as separate issues filed when this lands.

## Explicit out of scope

- ML-based duplicate detection (same as #59 out-of-scope list)
- Cross-repo issue resolution
- Timeline events beyond closing-PR references

## AC

- [ ] Per-issue `closing_prs` field present (array, possibly empty) on every snapshot issue
- [ ] Default path still a single `gh` invocation (GraphQL query + pagination counts as one logical fetch)
- [ ] `--with-comments` flag opt-in; documented N-API-call cost; fetches concurrency-bounded
- [ ] `--with-closed-issues` flag opt-in; caps via config
- [ ] Existing schema tests updated; new tests cover `closing_prs` presence via stubbed GraphQL response
- [ ] `sync-pull.js` behavior unchanged (its fetch path can continue using the pre-existing `gh issue list` pattern for backwards compat, or migrate if straightforward)
